Period Pain 
URL of this page: https://medlineplus.gov/periodpain.html 
Period Pain
Also called: Dysmenorrhea, Menstrual cramps, Menstrual pain 
Summary 
What are painful periods? 
Menstruation , or period, is normal vaginal bleeding that happens as part of a woman's monthly cycle. Many women have painful periods, also called dysmenorrhea. The pain is most often menstrual cramps, which are a throbbing, cramping pain in your lower abdomen. You may also have other symptoms, such as lower back pain, nausea, diarrhea, and headaches. Period pain is not the same
